intro: This app helps **beginners** understand the basic formula behind **cantilever beam tip deflection** — a fundamental concept in **MEMS (Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems)** and micro-actuator design.

schematic.title: Cantilever Beam Model
schematic.caption: The beam is **clamped on the left** (no movement, no rotation) and **free on the right**, where the force is applied.

si_note: All calculations are performed internally using **SI units** (m, Pa, N). Slider inputs in μm, GPa, and μN are converted automatically.

assumptions.title: Model Assumptions
assumptions.rectangular: **Rectangular cross-section** — the beam has a uniform rectangular profile.
assumptions.small_deformation: **Small deformation** — deflection is small relative to beam length.
assumptions.linear_elastic: **Linear elastic behavior** — material obeys Hooke's law, no plastic deformation.

chart.title: Effect of Applied Force on Cantilever Tip Deflection
chart.caption: The deflection increases **linearly** with force — double the force means double the deflection (for a given beam geometry).

limitations.title: Limitations
limitations.no_nonlinear: **No nonlinear deformation** — the model does not account for large-deflection (geometric nonlinearity) effects.
limitations.no_damping: **No damping** — the model is purely static; it does not include viscous or structural damping.
limitations.no_dynamic: **No dynamic response** — the model does not simulate vibration, resonance, or time-dependent loading.

fem_disclaimer: **This is a simplified educational model, not a replacement for Finite Element Method (FEM) simulation.**

validation.range_L: Length L must be between 50 and 1000 μm.
validation.range_w: Width w must be between 5 and 200 μm.
validation.range_t: Thickness t must be between 1 and 50 μm.
validation.range_E: Young's Modulus E must be between 1 and 250 GPa.
validation.range_F: Force F must be between 0.10 and 100.00 μN.
